hadiths.ar:
1183  -  وَّ ق َّذْ رُّوِى وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ ق ِى  صَّلاَّة ِ 
إَّلْعَّصْزِ ج َّعَّلَّ إَّلْأ َّو َّلَّي َّي ْن ِ ن َّاق ِلَّه ً وَّ إَّلْأ َّج ِي زَّي َّي ْن ِ ق َّزِي ص َّه ً .
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated: "If a person fears for himself due to those praying with 
him, he should pray the last two rak'ahs and make them voluntary (nafl) prayers”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 398 • Wasail Al-Shia, V8, p. 400 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, 
V6, p. 467 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1182
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٨٢

hadiths.ar:
1184  - وَّ ق َّذْ رُّوِى صَّلاَّة ِ إَّلط  ُّهْزِ ج َّعَّلَّ إَّلْأ َّو َّلَّي َّي ْن ِ إَّلط  ُّهْزَّ وَّ إَّلْأ َّج ِي زَّي َّي ْن ِ إَّلْعَّصْزَّ . 
وهذ ة إلأ ج ن ار لي سث  مح ي لق ه  وإلمصلى  ن الح ي ار ن ا ي ها إ ج ذ  ج ار  .
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated: "If it is during the Dhuhr prayer, he should make the first 
two rak'ahs obligatory and the last two rak'ahs voluntary.  
If it is during the Asr prayer, he should make the first two rak'ahs voluntary and the last 
two rak'ahs obligatory." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 398 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1258 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, 
p. 400 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 6, p. 467 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1183
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٨٣

hadiths.ar:
1185  -  وَّ رَّوَّى عَّن ْذُّ إَّلل َّهِ ن ْن إَّلْمُّع ِي زَّة ِ ق َّالَّ كَّان َّ مَّن ْصُّورُّ ن ْن ُّ جَّار ِمٍ ي َّق ُّول 
رَّكْعَّي َّي ْن ِ ق َّكَّن ِّزْ ي ُّم َّ إِج ْلِسْ ق َّا ِذ َّإ ق ُّمْث َّ ق َّكَّن ِّزْ .
hadiths.en:
It has been narrated: "If it is during the Dhuhr prayer, he should make the first 
two rak'ahs for Dhuhr and the last two rak'ahs for Asr."  
[AL SADUQ] 
These narrations are not contradictory, and the person praying has the option to follow 
any of these practices, and it is permissible. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 398 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1259 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, 
p. 400 • Mustadrak Al-Wasail, Vol. 6, p. 467 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1184
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٨٤

hadiths.ar:
1186  - وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  « . 
ومن  صلى مح الف  ق ق زإ  إلسح ذة  ولم ي سح ذ ق لي و م ن زإ سه . وإ ذ  ق ال إلأم " سمغ إلله لمن  جمذة "  ق ال إلذ ي ن  ج لق ه إلحمذ لله 
رب  إلعالمي ن  "  وي ح ق ص ون  إ صوإي هم، وإ ن  كان  معهم ق ال: " رن ن ا لك  إلحمذ .
hadiths.en:
Abdullah ibn al-Mughira narrated that Mansur ibn Hazim used to say:  
"If you come to the Imam and he is sitting, having already prayed two rak'ahs, say the 
takbir (Allahu Akbar) and then sit. When you stand up, say the takbir again."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1185
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٨٥

hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said: "It is sufficient for you to recite 
like a whisper to yourself when you are with them (in congregation)."  
[AL SADUQ] 
If someone is praying with those who differ in practice and recites a verse of 
prostration but does not prostrate, they should bow their head as a gesture.  
When the Imam says, "Sami' Allahu liman hamidah (Allah {SWT} hears the one who 
praises Him)," those behind him should say, "Alhamdulillahi Rabbil 'Alamin (All praise 
is due to Allah {SWT}, the Lord {AZJ} of all worlds)" but in a low voice.  
If the person is praying with them, they should say, "Rabbana laka al -hamd (Our Lord 
{AZJ}, to You is the praise)." 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 315 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 399 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 2, p. 97 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 3, p. 36 • Al -Istibsar, Part 1, p. 321 • Al -Istibsar, Part 1, p. 430 • Al -
Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 690 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1207 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 128 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 364 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1186
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٨٦

hadiths.ar: 1188  - وَّ رَّوَّى إ َّن ُّو ن َّصِي زٍ عَّن ْ إ َّجَّذِهِمَّا عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
The Messenger of Allah {saws} said: "Whoever leads a group in prayer and 
reserves the supplication for himself, excluding them, has betrayed them”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 400 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, Part 3, p. 281 • Al -Wafi, Vol. 
8, p. 1264 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 7, p. 106 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 425 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1187
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٨٧

hadiths.ar:
1189  -  وَّ ق َّذْ رُّوِى َّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  ن َّكْزِ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  سَّم َّالٍ ق َّالَّ: صَّل َّي ْث ُّ ج َّلْف َّ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إَّلْق ح ْزَّ ق َّلَّم َّا ق َّزَّع َّ مِن ْ 
ق ِزَّإءَّي ِهِ ق ِى  إَّلي  َّان ِي َّه ِ ج َّهَّزَّ ن ِصَّوْي ِهِ ن َّحْوإً مِم َّا كَّان َّ ي َّق ْزَّإ ُّ وَّق َّالَّ «إَّلل َّهُّم َّ إِع ْق ِزْ لَّن َّا وَّ إِرْجَّمْن َّا وَّ عَّاق ِن َّا وَّ إُّعْف ُّ عَّن  َّا ق ِى  إَّلذ ُّن ْي َّا 
وَّ إَّلْأ ج ِزَّة ِ .
hadiths.en:
Abu Basir narrated from one of the Imams {a.s} who said:  
"Do not let the Imam hear your supplication behind him”.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 400 • Al-Wafi, V. 8, p. 1205 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1188
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٨٨

hadiths.ar:
1190  -  وَّ رَّوَّى جَّق ْضُّ ن ْن ُّ إَّلْن َّح ْي َّزِى ِّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّال 
ج َّلْق َّهُّ صَّلاَّي َّهُّمْ وَّ ي َّن ْن َّع ِى  لِلْا ِمَّامِ إ َّن ْ ي ُّسْمِغَّ مَّن ْ ج َّلْق َّهُّ إَّلي  َّس َّه ُّذَّ وَّ لأَّ ي ُّسْمِعُّون َّهُّ هُّمْ س َّي ْن اً ي َّعْ ن ِى  إَّلس  َّهَّاذَّي َّي ْن ِ وَّ ي ُّسْمِعَّهُّمْ 
إ َّي ْص اً إَّلس َّلاَّ مُّ عَّلَّي ْن َّا وَّ عَّلَّى عِن َّاذِ إَّلل َّهِ إَّلص َّالِحِي ن َّ .
hadiths.en:
Abu Bakr ibn Abi Samal who said:  
"I prayed behind Abu Abdullah {a.s} for Fajr (dawn prayer). When he finished the 
recitation in the second rak'ah, he raised his voice slightly as if reciting and said:  
'O’ Allah {SWT}, forgive us, have mercy on us, grant us well -being, and pardon us in this 
world and the Hereafter. Truly, You have power over all things.'".  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 400 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 6, p. 275 • Wasail Al-Shia, 
Vol. 6, p. 291 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1189
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٨٩

hadiths.ar:
1191  -  وَّق َّال إَّلص َّاذِق ُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ  إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : «وي َّعَّالَّى
ج َّذ ُّك َّ وَّ هَّذ َّإ س َّى ْء  ق َّالَّي ْهُّ إَّلْح ِن  ُّ ن ِح َّهَّالَّه ٍ ق َّحَّكَّاةُّ إَّلل َّهُّ ي َّعَّالَّى عَّن ْهَّا وَّ ن ِق َّوْلِهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ عَّلَّي ْن َّ ا وَّ عَّلَّى عِن َّاذِ إَّلل َّهِ إَّلص َّالِحِي ن َّ . 
ي عن ى  ق ى  إلي س هذ إلأ ول، وإ ما ق ى  إلي س هذ إلي ان ى  ن عذ إلس هاذي ي ن  ق لا ن ا س ن ه لأن  إلمصلى  إ ذ إ ي س هذ إلس هاذي ي ن  ق ى  إلي س هذ  
إلأ ج ي ز ق ق ذ ق زع  من  إلصلاة  .
hadiths.en:
Hafs ibn al -Bakhtari narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
who said: "It is appropriate for the Imam to sit until those behind him complete their 
prayers. The Imam should also recite the tashahhud (testimony of faith) loud enough 
for those behind him to hear, but they should not make their recitation audible to h im 
- referring to the two testimonies (Shahadatayn). He should also audibly recite 'Peace 
be upon us and upon the righteous servants of Allah {SWT}.'" 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1190
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٩٠

hadiths.ar:
1192   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ إ َّج َّاةُّ مُّوسَّى ن ْن َّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ِ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ي َّكُّون ُّ ج َّلْف َّ إ ِمَّامٍ ق َّي َّطُّولُّ ق ِى   
إَّلي  َّس َّه ُّذِ ق َّي َّا ْج ُّذ ُّةُّ إَّلْن َّوْلُّ إ َّوْ ي َّح َّاف ُّ عَّلَّى س َّى ْءٍ إ َّن ْ ي َّق ُّوب َّ إ َّوْ ي َّعْزِض ُّ لَّهُّ وَّج َّغ  كَّي ْف َّ ي َّصْن َّغُّ ق َّالَّ  «
ي َّذَّعُّ إَّلْأ ِمَّام .  
وعلى إلأمام إ ن  لأ ي ق وم من  مصلاة جي ى ي ي م من  ج لق ه إلصلاة ، ق ا ن  ق ام ق لا س ى  علي ه. وق ال إ ن ى    رجمه إلله  ق ى  رسالي ه إ لى : 
إ ن  ج زج ث  من ك  ري ح إ و ع ي زها مما ي ن ق ض  إلوص وء إ و ذ كزب  إ ن ك  على ع ي ز وص وء ق سلم ق ى  إ ى  جال كن ث  ق ى  إلصلاة  وق ذم  
رج لا ي صلى  ن الق وم ن ق ي ه  صلاي هم وي وض  وإ عذ صلاي ك  .
hadiths.en:
Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said:  
"Ibn Mas'ud corrupted people's prayers with two things: by saying 'Blessed be Your 
Name, and Exalted is Your Majesty,' which was something the jinn said ignorantly, and 
Allah {SWT}, the Exalted, narrated it from them, and by saying 'Peace be upon us and 
upon the righteous servants of Allah {SWT}'." 
[AL SADUQ] 
This refers to the first tashahhud. However, in the second tashahhud after the two 
testimonies (Shahadatayn), there is no problem because when the worshipper 
completes the two testimonies in the final tashahhud, they have concluded their 
prayer. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1191
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٩١

hadiths.ar:
1193  - وَّق َّال إ َّمِي زُّ إَّلْمُّو ْمِن ِي ن َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ : «
إ َّوْ رَّعَّف َّ رُّعَّاق اً إ َّوْ إ َّر  َّ إ َّر  إً ق ِى  ن َّطْن ِهِ ق َّلْي َّح ْعَّلْ ي َّوْن َّهُّ عَّلَّى إ َّن ْق ِهِ ي ُّم َّ لْي َّن ْصَّزِف ْ وَّ لْي َّا ْج ُّذ ْ ن ِي ذِ رَّج ُّلٍ ق َّلْي ُّصَّلِّ مَّكَّان َّهُّ ي ُّ م َّ لْي َّي َّوَّص  َّا ْ  
وَّ لْي ُّي ِم َّ مَّا سَّن َّق َّهُّ ن ِهِ مِن َّ إَّلص َّلاَّة ِ وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّان َّ ج ُّن ُّن اً ق َّلْي َّع ْي َّسِلْ وَّ لْي ُّصَّلِّ إَّلص َّلاَّة َّ كُّل َّهَّا .
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -Kadhim {a.s} about a 
man who is behind an Imam and the Imam prolongs the tashahhud, causing the man 
to feel the urge to urinate, fear missing something, or experience pain. What should he 
do?  
Imam {a.s} replied: "He should perform the taslim (salutation of peace), leave, and leave 
the Imam." 
[AL SADUQ] 
The Imam should not leave his place of prayer until those behind him complete their 
prayers, but if he does stand up, there is no blame on him.  
My father, may Allah {SWT} have mercy on him, wrote to me in his letter:  
If wind or anything else that breaks wudu' comes from you, or you remember that you 
are not in a state of wudu', then say the Taslim, leave, and appoint someone else to 
lead the congregation in completing the prayer. Then, perform wudu' and repeat your 
prayer.  
[REFERENCES] Questions of Ali bin Jaafar, Part 1, p. 178 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 401 • Tahdhib 
Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 283 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1192
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٩٢

hadiths.ar:
1194  -  وَّ رَّوَّى مُّعَّاوِي َّه ُّ ن ْن ُّ مَّي ْسَّزَّة َّ عَّن ِ إَّلص َّاذِق ِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال جْذَّب َّ إ َّن ْ ي ُّق َّذِّمَّ إ ِلأ َّ مَّن ْ 
إ َّذْرَّك َّ إَّلْأ ِق َّامَّه َّ ق َّا ِن ْ ق َّذ َّمَّ مَّسْن ُّوق اً ن ِزَّكْعَّه ٍ   رَّوَّى عَّن ْهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال 
ق َّلْي ُّومِ إ ِلَّي ْهِمْ ي َّمِي ن اً وَّ س ِمَّالأً ق َّلْي َّن ْصَّزِق ُّوإ ي ُّم َّ لْي ُّكَّمِّلْ هُّوَّ مَّا ق َّاي َّهُّ مِن ْ صَّلاَّي ِهِ .
hadiths.en:
Commander of the Faithful Imam Ali {a.s} said:  
"If an Imam leading the prayer realizes during the prayer that he is in a state of janabah 
(major ritual impurity) due to forgetfulness, or if he experiences a minor impurity, 
nosebleed, or a disturbance in his stomach, he should place his garment over his nose 
and leave the prayer.  
He should take the hand of a man to lead the prayer in his place, then perform wudu' 
and complete what remains of his prayer.  
If he is in a state of janabah, he should perform ghusl (ritual purification bath) and then 
repeat the entire prayer." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 402 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 426 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1193
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٩٣

hadiths.ar:
1195 - وَّ رَّوَّى ج َّمِي لُّ ن ْن ُّ ذَّر َّإح ٍ عَّن ْهُّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم : ق ِى  رَّج ُّلٍ إ َّم َّ ق َّوْماً عَّلَّى ع َّي ْزِ وُّص ُّوءٍ ق َّان ْصَّزَّف َّ وَّ ق َّذ َّمَّ رَّج ُّلاً وَّ لَّمْ 
ي َّذْرِ إَّلْمُّق َّذ َّمُّ مَّا صَّل َّى إَّلْأ ِمَّامُّ ق َّن ْلَّهُّ ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Muawiya ibn Maysara narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
who said:  
"It is not appropriate for the Imam, if he becomes impure (breaks his wudu'), to appoint 
someone else to lead except for someone who was present from the beginning of the 
iqamah (call to start the prayer). If he appoints someone who has missed a rak'ah, it  
should not be done."  
Abdullah ibn Sinan narrated from him {a.s} that He said:  
"When the Imam completes his prayer with the congregation, he should gesture to 
them to the right and left so they can disperse, and then he should complete whatever 
part of the prayer he missed."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 402 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1240 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1194
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٩٤

hadiths.ar:
1196  -  وَّق َّال ر ُّرَّإرَّة ُّ لِأ َّن ِى  ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ :  رَّج ُّل  ذَّج َّلَّ مَّغَّ ق َّوْمٍ ق ِى  صَّلاَّي ِهِمْ وَّ هُّوَّ لأَّ ي َّن ْوِي هَّا صَّلاَّة ً وَّ إ َّجْذَّب َّ 
إ ِمَّامُّهُّمْ ق َّا َّج َّذ َّ ن ِي َّذِ ذ َّلِك َّ إَّلز َّج ُّلِ ق َّق َّذ َّمَّهُّ ق َّصَّل َّى ن ِهِمْ إ َّ ي ُّح ْز ِي هِمْ صَّلاَّي ُّهُّمْ ن ِصَّلاَّي ِهِ وَّ هُّوَّ لأَّ ي َّن ْوِي هَّا صَّلاَّة ً ق َّال 
لِلز َّ ج ُّلِ إ َّن ْ ي َّذْج ُّلَّ مَّغَّ ق َّوْمٍ ق ِى  صَّلاَّي ِهِمْ وَّ هُّوَّ لأَّ ي َّن ْوِي هَّا صَّلاَّة ً ن َّلْ ي َّن ْن َّع ِى  لَّهُّ إ َّن ْ ي َّن ْوِي َّهَّا وَّ إ ِن ْ كَّ ان َّ ق َّذْ صَّل َّى ق َّا ِن  َّ لَّهُّ 
صَّلاَّة ً إ ُّج ْزَّى وَّ إ ِلأ َّ ق َّلاَّ ي َّذْج ُّلَّن  َّ مَّعَّهُّمْ وَّ ق َّذْ ي ُّح ْز ِى  عَّن ِ إَّلْق َّوْمِ صَّلاَّي ُّهُّمْ وَّ إ ِ ن ْ لَّمْ ي َّن ْوِهَّا .
hadiths.en:
Jamil ibn Darraj narrated from Imam {a.s} regarding a man who led a 
congregation in prayer without wudu' (ritual purity), then left and appointed another 
man to lead, but the new leader did not know what the previous Imam had prayed.  
Imam {a.s} said: "Those behind him should remind him." 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 403 • Al-Wafi, V. 8, p. 1240 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1195
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٩٥

hadiths.ar:
1197  -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّج َّاةُّ مُّوسَّى ن ْن َّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ْ إ ِمَّامٍ إ َّجْذَّب َّ وَّ إِن ْصَّزَّف َّ وَّ 
لَّمْ ي ُّق َّذِّمْ إ َّجَّذإً مَّا جَّالُّ إَّلْق َّوْمِ ق َّال لْي ُّي ِم َّ ن ِهِمْ مَّا ن َّق ِى َّ مِن ْهَّا وَّ ق َّذْ ي َّم َّث ْ  
صَّلاَّي ُّهُّمْ .
hadiths.en:
Zurarah asked Imam Al -Baqir {a.s} about a man who joined a group in their 
prayer without intending it as his own prayer. Then, their Imam experienced an issue 
(e.g., impurity) and took the hand of that man and appointed him to lead the prayer.  
Zurarah asked: "Does their prayer count if they complete it with him, even though he 
did not intend it as his own prayer?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "It is not appropriate for a man to join a group in their prayer without 
intending it as his own prayer. Rather, he should intend it as such, even if he has 
already prayed. This way, he will have another prayer counted. If not, he should not 
join them. Nevertheless, the prayer of the group may be valid even if he did not intend 
it as his own”. 
[REFERENCES] Al-Kafi, Volume 3, p. 382 • Man La Yahduruhu Al -Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 403 • Tahdhib Al -Ahkam, 
Part 3, p. 41 • Al-Wafi, Vol. 8, p. 1241 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 376 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1196
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٩٦

hadiths.ar:
1198  -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّم :  إ َّن  َّهُّ سُّن ِلَّ عَّن ْ رَّج ُّلٍ إ َّم َّ ق َّوْماً وَّ صَّل َّى ن ِهِمْ رَّكْعَّه ً ي ُّم َّ مَّاب َّ ق َّالَّ  
« صَّل َّى ن ِق َّوْمٍ وَّ هُّوَّ ج ُّن ُّث 
إ َّوْ عَّلَّى ع َّ ي ْزِ وُّص ُّوءٍ ق َّعَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلْأ ِعَّاذَّة ُّ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ عَّلَّي ْهِمْ إ َّن ْ ي ُّعِي ذُّوإ وَّ لَّي ْسَّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إ َّن ْ ي ُّعْلِمَّهُّمْ وَّ لَّوْ كَّان َّ   ذ َّلِك َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ لَّهَّلَّك 
ق َّالَّ ق ُّلْث ُّ كَّي ْف َّ كَّان َّ  ي َّصْن َّغُّ ن ِمَّن ْ ق َّذْ ج َّزَّح َّ إ ِلَّى ج ُّزَّإسَّان َّ وَّ كَّي ْف َّ كَّان َّ ي َّصْن َّغُّ ن ِمَّن ْ لأَّ ي َّعْزِف ُّ ق َّال .
hadiths.en:
Ali ibn Ja'far asked his brother Imam Musa ibn Jafar Al -Kadhim {a.s} about an 
Imam who becomes impure (invalidates his wudu') and leaves the prayer without 
appointing someone to continue leading. What is the status of the congregation?  
Imam {a.s} replied: "There is no valid prayer for them without an Imam. Therefore, one of 
them should step forward to lead the remainder of the prayer, and their prayer will be 
completed." 
[REFERENCES] Questions of Ali bin Jaafar, Part 1, p. 256 • Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 403 • Tahdhib 
Al-Ahkam, Part 3, p. 283 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1197
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٩٧

hadiths.ar:
1199   -  وَّ رَّوَّى إَّلْحَّلَّن ِى  ُّ عَّن ْ إ َّن ِى  عَّن ْذِ إَّلل َّهِ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ إ َّن  َّهُّ ق َّال ق َّاج ْعَّلْ إ َّو َّلَّ صَّلاَّي ِك َّ 
مَّا إِسْي َّق ْن َّلْث َّ مِن ْهَّا وَّ لأَّ ي َّح ْعَّلْ إ َّو َّلَّ صَّلاَّي ِك َّ إ ج ِزَّهَّا .  
ومن  إ ج لسه إلأمام ق ى  موص غ ي ح ث  إ ن  ي ق وم ق ي ه ي ح اق ى  وإ ق عى  إ ق عاء ولم ي ح لس ممكن ا .
hadiths.en:
Al-Halabi narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} that he was 
asked about a man who led a congregation, prayed one rak'ah with them, and then 
passed away.  
Imam {a.s} said: "They should appoint another man to lead them, count the rak'ah 
(already prayed), and move the deceased to the back. Those who touched him must 
perform ghusl (ritual purification).  
If someone led a congregation while in a state of major ritual impurity (janabah) or 
without wudu', they must repeat their prayer, but the congregation does not need to 
repeat theirs, and the Imam is not obligated to inform them. If he had to inform them, 
it would cause great distress."  
I asked: "How would this apply to someone who went to Khurasan or to those who are 
unknown?"  
Imam {a.s} replied: "This is not applicable to him (in such a case)."  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1198
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٩٨

hadiths.ar:
1200  -  وروى عن ي ذ ن ن  ر رإرة  عن  إ ن ى  عن ذ إلله علي ه إلسلام " ق ى  رج ل ذج ل مغ إلأمام ق ى  إلصلاة  وق ذ سن ق ه 
ن زكعه  ق لما ق زع  إلأمام ج زح  مغ إلن اس، ي م ذ كز إ ن ه ق اي ي ه ركعه ، ق ال: ي عي ذ ركعه  وإجذة  .
hadiths.en:
Al-Halabi narrated from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} that He said:  
"If you miss a part of the prayer with the Imam, consider what you perform upon joining 
as the beginning of your prayer, and do not consider the beginning of your prayer as its 
end”. 
[AL SADUQ] 
If the Imam makes you sit at a place where you should stand, you should sit lightly 
(without fully resting) and not sit in a fully settled manner.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 404 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 386 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1199
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١١٩٩

hadiths.ar:
1201  -  وَّ ق ِى  كِي َّاب ِ ر ِي َّاذِ ن ْن ِ مَّزْوَّإن َّ إَّلْق َّن ْذِى ِّ وَّ ق ِى ن َّوَّإذِرِ مُّحَّم َّذِ ن ْن ِ إ َّن ِى  عُّمَّي ْزٍ إ َّن  َّ إَّلص َّاذِق َّ عَّلَّي ْهِ إَّلس َّلاَّمُّ ق َّالَّ: ق ِى   
رَّج ُّلٍ صَّل َّى ن ِق َّوْمٍ مِن ْ جِي ن َّ ج َّزَّج ُّوإ مِن ْ ج ُّزَّإسَّان َّ جَّي  َّى ق َّذِمُّوإ مَّك َّه َّ ق َّا ِذ َّإ هُّوَّ ي َّهُّوذِى    إ َّوْ ن َّصْزَّإن ِى     ق َّال 
إ ِعَّاذَّة   .  
وسمعث  ج ماعه  من  مس اي ح ن ا ي ق لون : إ ن ه لي س علي هم إ عاذة  س ى  مما ج هز ق ي ه وعلي هم إ عاذة  ما صلى ن هم مما لم ي ح هز ق ي ه، 
وإلحذي ث  إلمق صل ي حكم على إلمح مل .
hadiths.en:
Ubayd ibn Zurarah reported from Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al -Sadiq {a.s} 
regarding a man who joined the prayer with the Imam but had missed one rak'ah. After 
the Imam concluded the prayer, he left with the people and then remembered that he 
had missed a rak'ah.  
Imam {a.s} said: "He should perform one rak'ah”. 
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1, p. 405 • Wasail Al-Shia, Vol. 8, p. 374 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1200
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١٢٠٠

hadiths.ar:
1202   -  وَّ سَّا َّلَّ عَّلِى  ُّ ن ْن ُّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ إ َّج َّاةُّ مُّوسَّى ن ْن َّ ج َّعْق َّزٍ عَّلَّي ْهِمَّا إَّلس َّلاَّم :  عَّن ِ إَّلْمَّزْإ َّة ِ ي َّو ُّم ُّ إَّلن ِّسَّاءَّ مَّا جَّذ ُّ رَّق ْغِ صَّوْي ِهَّا 
ن ِالي  َّكْن ِي زِ وَّ إَّلْق ِزَّإءَّة ِ ق َّق َّال .
hadiths.en:
In the book of Ziyad ibn Marwan al -Qandi and in the Nawadir of Muhammad 
ibn Abi Umayr, it is narrated that Imam Jafar ibn Muhammad Al-Sadiq {a.s} said regarding 
a man who led a group in prayer from the time they left Khurasan until they reached 
Mecca, and then it was discovered that he was a Jew or a Christian:  
"They do not need to repeat their prayers”. 
[AL SADUQ] 
I also heard a group of our elders say: They are not required to repeat any prayers in 
which he recited aloud, but they must repeat those prayers in which he led them 
silently. The detailed hadith takes precedence over the general statement.  
[REFERENCES] Man La Yahduruhu Al-Faqih, Vol. 1 
﴿HADITH ﴾

reference.citation: Man Lā Yaḥḍuruh al-Faqīh, Vol. 1, Ch. 56, H. 1201
reference.location: Volume 1 › CONGREGATIONAL PRAYER AND ITS VIRTUE
reference.ar_citation: من لا يحضره الفقيه، باب ٥٦، ح ١٢٠١
